name: Fishbowl Inventory Management API
what_it_does: Fishbowl Inventory REST API for SMB manufacturing and warehouse inventory management with QuickBooks integration. Enables AI agents to manage inventory tracking and stock level monitoring for SMB inventory automation, handle purchase order creation and vendor management for procurement workflow, access work order and manufacturing operations management for light manufacturing automation, retrieve sales order and fulfillment processing for order management, manage multi-location warehouse and bin-level inventory tracking for warehouse operations, handle lot and serial number tracking for regulated industry inventory compliance, access inventory valuation and COGS calculation for financial accounting integration, retrieve barcode scanning and receiving for warehouse automation, manage cycle count and physical inventory reconciliation for inventory accuracy, and integrate Fishbowl with QuickBooks, Shopify, Amazon, and business platforms.
use_cases: Tracking inventory from SMB operations agents
Processing purchase orders from procurement agents
Managing work orders from light manufacturing agents
Reconciling inventory from cycle count agents
not_for: Enterprise manufacturing requiring full ERP (use Epicor or Infor)
High-volume e-commerce fulfillment without manufacturing component
best_when: An SMB manufacturer, distributor, or retailer using QuickBooks wanting AI agents to automate inventory management, purchase orders, work orders, multi-location warehouse tracking, and fulfillment with native QuickBooks financial integration.
avoid_when: QUICKBOOKS SYNC DEPENDENCY: Fishbowl inventory data and QuickBooks financial data maintain bidirectional sync; automated Fishbowl operations without monitoring QuickBooks sync health can create inventory-to-financials reconciliation breaks; automated workflows must validate sync status before financial close. LOT/SERIAL TRACEABILITY FOR RECALL: Automated lot-tracked inventory management must maintain complete lot genealogy for FDA/USDA product recall; automated lot-number assignment without source-to-ship traceability creates recall response failure. HAZMAT INVENTORY COMPLIANCE: Automated inventory management for chemical or hazardous materials must comply with EPA SARA Title III reporting thresholds; automated inventory records for hazmat must be accurate for Tier II reporting. Fishbowl licensing model — Fishbowl on-premise installation requires per-concurrent-user licensing; automated agent access must use dedicated licensed user session; unlicensed automated API access violates Fishbowl EULA.

auth.notes: Fishbowl uses API key and session-based authentication. REST API with JSON/XML. Orem, Utah HQ. Founded 2001. Private. SMB inventory and manufacturing software. 20,000+ SMB customers. QuickBooks inventory add-on market leader. On-premise and cloud deployment. Competes with Cin7, DEAR Inventory, and Acctivate for QuickBooks inventory management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — perpetual license with annual maintenance or subscription
pricing.paid_tiers: Perpetual per-user license or annual subscription; manufacturing and warehouse editions
pricing.notes: Orem UT. Founded 2001. Private. 20,000+ SMB customers. QuickBooks inventory leader. On-premise and cloud. Perpetual or subscription licensing per concurrent user.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits based on licensed concurrent user sessions; on-premise performance varies by server hardware

known_agent_gotchas: NO WEBHOOKS — POLLING REQUIRED: Fishbowl lacks webhook support; inventory change, PO receipt, and work order completion events require polling; implement scheduled polling with appropriate interval for SMB operations cadence

QUICKBOOKS SYNC BREAK DETECTION: Automated Fishbowl inventory operations must monitor QuickBooks sync health; Fishbowl-QuickBooks sync breaks silently when connection credentials expire; automated workflows continuing on broken sync create inventory-financial reconciliation errors requiring manual correction

On-premise API network dependency — Fishbowl on-premise installation requires network access to Fishbowl server; cloud-hosted automated agents must use VPN or API gateway to reach on-premise Fishbowl; plan network architecture before cloud automation

Concurrent user session licensing — Fishbowl API access requires a licensed concurrent user session; automated agent must use a dedicated licensed session; unlicensed session access violates EULA and creates connection failures when licensed seats are exhausted

LOT genealogy for regulated inventory — automated lot-tracked inventory management must maintain complete lot-to-parent genealogy for FDA recall traceability; automated lot assignment without source purchase order and vendor lot linkage creates incomplete recall traceability

Work order explosion accuracy — automated work order creation for finished goods must accurately explode BOM (Bill of Materials) to component requirements; automated work orders with stale BOM data create material shortage and production failure
agent_readiness.security_notes: SMB inventory management. SOC2. API key. US. Inventory, manufacturing, and QuickBooks financial data.

security_score_components.security_notes: SMB inventory. SOC2. API key. US. Inventory and manufacturing data.
